key_insight: Coil geometry and support clamp placement can be optimized together with differentiable FEA to reduce stress without sacrificing magnetic field quality.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more practical stellarator coil design by integrating structural integrity into the electromagnetic optimization loop instead of relying on manual post hoc support placement.
method: Built coil-fem, an open-source differentiable structural mechanics tool for stellarator design
Coupled support-aware FEA into the coil optimization loop
Added a penalty on coil stress while preserving field accuracy
evidence_signal: Compared with an unoptimized baseline, the optimized design achieved 2.4x lower RMS von Mises stress with similar magnetic field error.
watch_item: The approach still depends on accurate surrogate/FEA modeling and may require careful validation before transfer to full reactor-scale hardware.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
